element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early game attack, lightsaber-style melee weapon always available
Planet navigation/exit tool, essential utility item
Ability to comprehend alien text messages, included in manual narrative
Health/energy progression mechanic, one per planet to discover
Ranged weapon upgrade, expands combat options
Enhanced melee weapon upgrade from Laser Sword
Specialized weapon tied to Sauria planet
Spatial manipulation tool for navigation shortcuts
Environmental mechanic where protagonist drawn upside-down, enemies inverted

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Multi-stage pacing across eight planets with escalating mechanical complexity; early stages teach core sword combat, mid-stages introduce ranged weapons, late stages feature environmental manipulation (gravity reversal) and specialized tools

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Teleport Location shortcuts suggest hidden spawn points and out-of-bounds exploration encouraged; game designed to reward bug-finding and sequence breaking
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Gravity-affected level design (Turvia with reversed gravity) signals non-standard spatial rules; planet selection ship interface suggests non-linear world traversal

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to sword combat and basic platforming on initial planets; establishes core movement and attack mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon diversification (ranged options introduced); multi-planet exploration with varied environmental hazards; gravity mechanics become relevant
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex environmental challenges; gravity reversal on Turvia; specialized weapons and teleportation tools required; door maze on final stage features out-of-bounds exploit detection that causes freeze if sequence broken
notable_spikes: Turvia planet: gravity reversal creates inverted spatial reference for player and enemies
Final stage: door maze with cheat detection and freeze state punishment for out-of-bounds attempts

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight distinct planets (Cyberia, Sauria, Effluvia, Turvia, etc.) each containing unique Compuminds to locate and environmental hazards; non-linear progression through planet selection ship interface
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Turvia (gravity reversal), Sauria (jungle world), Cyberia (cyberpunk zone), Effluvia (trash/caustic world)

notable_level_patterns.description: Turvia features reversed gravity affecting entire visual presentation; player and enemies drawn upside-down; spatial orientation inverted but mechanics consistent
End-game door maze with deliberate out-of-bounds detection; attempting to cheat/exploit causes permanent freeze state; punishes sequence breaking
Teleport Locations with shortcut spawns near energy containers and key positions; suggests design that rewards exploration and memory of spawn mechanics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Volcania (energy container spawn), Sauria (dinosaur tail spawn), Odar (flying enemies spawn)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Rad Gravity article)
wikipedia (The Adventures of Rad Gravity article)
